name: Shadow Cover
description: A stealth modifier where darkness, cover volumes, and light sampling reduce how easily AI vision systems detect the player.
design_notes: Shadow cover depends on lighting data being legible to both player and AI. Define shadow_cover_id, light_level_sample points, moving light updates, cover volume modifiers, enemy vision cone interaction, and camera exposure. The mechanic should reward reading light and silhouette without making detection feel arbitrary.
design_purpose: Let players use darkness and occlusion as positional stealth tools.
Connect lighting, level design, AI vision, and player stance into one readable detection model.
Create movement choices around lit paths, dark routes, moving lights, and cover volumes.
player_fantasy: The player feels hidden in darkness, using light and silhouette as part of their tactical route.

parameters.name: shadow_cover_id
parameters.typical_range: volume id or sampled zone id
parameters.description: Identifier for the darkness or cover region affecting visibility.

parameters.name: light_sample_radius_units
parameters.description: Radius around the character used for light-level sampling.

parameters.name: visibility_modifier_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve mapping sampled light to AI visibility modifiers.

parameters.name: moving_light_refresh_ms
parameters.description: Refresh interval for flickering, moving, or scripted light sources.

parameters.name: enemy_vision_cone_multiplier
parameters.description: Detection multiplier applied while the player is in valid shadow cover.

parameters.name: exposure_probe_count
parameters.description: Number of body probes sampled for silhouette exposure.

edge_cases: Dynamic lights can move over the player while AI vision is between decision ticks.
Camera auto-exposure may make an area look dark to the player while AI light sampling reads it as bright.
Particles, muzzle flashes, or flashlights need rules for temporary visibility spikes.
Multiplayer stealth needs authority over light sampling so clients cannot hide by changing local graphics settings.
common_bugs: Light sampling reads only one foot point and misses the upper body in bright light.
Shadow cover volume remains active after a door opens or object moves away.
AI and UI use different visibility curves and disagree about whether the player is hidden.
Baked lighting data is used for moving lights, so detection never changes during patrol sweeps.
balancing_notes: Strong shadow protection supports classic stealth but can trivialize AI unless patrol routes include light pressure.
Graded visibility gives designers more tuning room than binary hidden/exposed states.
Readable light language matters more than photorealistic darkness for fair stealth.
accessibility_notes: Use UI state, outline strength, or icons in addition to subtle brightness differences.
Avoid requiring players to distinguish very dark tones without contrast, gamma, or visibility assists.

implementation_notes.unity: Sample light_level at multiple body probes or author cover volumes with shadow_cover_id. AI vision should read the same resolved visibility modifier as the player UI, and debug overlays should show probe results and active light sources.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area volumes, light masks, or sampled probes to calculate a visibility modifier. Update moving lights on a fixed interval and write the result into both visibility meter UI and AI perception state.
implementation_notes.unreal: Combine cover volumes, light probes, or Gameplay Tags with AI sight checks. Server-side detection should own visibility modifiers, while debug drawing shows probe positions, cone checks, and active shadow_cover_id.
implementation_notes.web: Represent shadow cover as sampled zones or tile/volume data rather than renderer brightness. The simulation computes visibility modifiers from light sources and cover volumes before AI detection runs.

relationships.reason: Shadow cover modifies visibility values based on light sampling and cover volumes.
Player light resources can reveal shadow cover and change stealth readability.
Shadow cover feedback needs contrast-safe indicators beyond dark color alone.

example_games.title: Thief: The Dark Project
example_games.note: Uses darkness as a major stealth readability layer.

example_games.title: Splinter Cell
example_games.note: Uses light and shadow as core stealth information.
